frontmatter.title: 7. AI and video
frontmatter.subtitle: Text-to-video, image-to-video, and the time problem

children.value: Image, text, and audio are all easier than
. A video is just a sequence of frames, but generating a sequence of frames that are
— same character, same lighting, same physics — is dramatically harder than generating any single frame.

By 2024 the first credible text-to-video systems arrived (Sora, Veo, Runway Gen-3, Kling, Pika, Luma). By 2026 they are good enough to do short cinematic clips, ad inserts, music videos, and B-roll for documentary work. They are still not good enough for sustained narrative film. This chapter looks at what is possible now and what is just over the horizon.
A 5-second clip at 30 fps is 150 frames. Even at compressed latent resolution, that is 100× the inference cost of a single image.
Each frame must be coherent with the previous one — same character, same scene, same lighting. This requires the model to “remember” what it just drew.
Cloth has to fall, water has to flow, hands have to grip. Image models can fake plausible physics in one frame; video models have to fake

children.alt: A schematic showing a stack of video frames over a time axis, with arrows showing temporal consistency requirements.

children.value: The hard part of video is not the frames; it is the time axis between them.
Modern video models use one of three strategies:
Each frame “sees” the others through cross-attention.
(space × space × time). Treat the whole clip as a single tensor; diffuse all at once. Conceptually clean, computationally expensive.
the frames in between using a separate, lighter model.
By 2026 the best public systems use combinations of all three.
What current video models can do

children.label: What current video models can do

children.value: In 2026, off-the-shelf tools (Sora, Runway, Veo, Kling, Pika, Luma) reliably produce:
— take a still and animate it.
— first frame + last frame → in-between motion.
— drive a static face image with an audio file.
Coherence falls off rapidly past 10–20 seconds.
Most commercial tools refuse explicit requests for real people, brands, or copyrighted characters.
Two people having a conversation is at the edge of reliable.
Same as image models, but in motion.
on signs and screens within a clip.
Video prompts are richer than image prompts because they include motion. A working template:

[Subject + key features],
[scene / setting],
[camera angle and movement],
[lighting and time of day],
[style],
[motion within the scene]

A young woman walking quickly along the riverbank in Oslo,
autumn leaves on the ground, river to the left,
medium-wide shot from a hand-held camera following her from behind,
overcast late-afternoon light,
documentary style,
she pulls a beanie out of her pocket and puts it on as she walks
: do not assume “looking sad” → “starts to cry”. Spell out the motion in time.
: most current systems generate a single shot. For multi-shot sequences you stitch in a video editor.
: 16:9 for landscape work; 9:16 for vertical (TikTok / Reels / Shorts).

The pieces of this pipeline that are most transformed by AI are the
. The traditional editorial work (pacing, sound design, colour) remains very human.
at the same time as the first. End-frame conditioning is often what separates “magic clip” from “useful clip”.
A specific genre of video AI worth flagging:

. Tools like HeyGen, Synthesia, D-ID, and many others let you upload (or pick from a stock library) a photograph or short clip of a person and drive it with a TTS voice. The output is a video of “that” person reading any text.
It is also a perfect tool for political and personal manipulation. As with voice cloning (chapter
explicit consent and clear labelling are non-negotiable
A short word on cost and access

children.label: A short word on cost and access

children.value: Video generation is still the most computationally expensive medium per second of output. As of 2026:
A 5-second 720p clip uses roughly the same compute as 100 image generations.
Most consumer tools price video in
; one clip costs 5–20 credits depending on length and quality.
Free tiers are very limited; the cheaper paid plans (around USD/EUR 10–20 per month) tend to be the right starting point for a course.
If your laptop has 16 GB of RAM and no dedicated GPU, do video work in a hosted tool. Local video generation is for workstations with serious GPUs.
A 10-second clip from a single image

children.label: A 10-second clip from a single image

Add an ambient soundtrack from chapter
Edit the three shots together in a video editor.
Watch it twice. Write down what works, what does not, and what you would re-do.
Pair up with another student. Each of you watches the other’s 30-second piece silently, then writes:
One thing the AI clearly produced well.
One thing that gives it away as AI.
One thing that would be the next step.
What kinds of moving-image work are easiest for current video AI? Which kinds remain stubbornly out of reach?
Watch one AI-generated short film online (search for “AI short film 2026”). Pause every two seconds. Where does coherence break down? Where does it hold?
Imagine a journalist using AI video for a news report. List three legitimate uses and three uses that would constitute serious misuse. What is the difference?
